In the 11th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1936), it was raised to the status of {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Festivals == * Main Festival : Held on April 25. * Koshiki Ageya Festival (Yōya Matsuri) == Subsidiary Shrine == * Tenman-gū == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)|version=81271364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:川田神社]] [[de:Kawata Jinja]] [[ja:川田神社]] [[ja:川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)]] {{wikidata link|Q11479034}} [[Category:Buildings in Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:pages]]

Haiden |Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka, Shiga}}{{ill|Minakuchi, Shiga|en|Minakuchi, Shiga|WD=Q3314706|lt=Minakuchi|1=Minakuchi, Shiga}} Kitauchitak 490 |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Yakawa|de|Ame-no-Yakawa|ja|天湯河板挙|lt=Ame-no-Yakawa|lt_ja=天湯川桁命|lt_de=Ame-no-Yakawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Yakawa|1=Ame-no-Yukawata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q52319907}}

Reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ikkensha-nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |OtherName = |Pilgrimage = |MainFestival = {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25|1=April 25}} |Ritual = Koshiki Ageya Festival (February 20) }} '''Kawata Jinja''' (川田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka, Shiga}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Yakawa|de|Ame-no-Yakawa|ja|天湯河板挙|lt=Ame-no-Yakawa|lt_ja=天湯川桁命|lt_de=Ame-no-Yakawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Yakawa|1=Ame-no-Yukawata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q52319907}} – Ancestor deity of the Tottori-no-Muraji * Amekawatana no Mikoto (Enshrined together) * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|de|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Onamuchi no Mikoto|de|Onamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} == Shrine Crest == * Trailing wisteria (下り藤) == History == According to tradition, it was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. Listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|de|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|de|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is one of the ''ronsha'' (theories) regarding that identification. In the 11th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} (1726), the shrine was elevated to Shōichii (正一位) in {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shinkai|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It served as the ''sōja'' of {{ill|Kurata-shō|de|Kurata-shō|ja|蔵田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurata-shō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1876), it was designated a ''sonsha'' ({{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}), then became a ''gōsha'' ({{ill|town shrine|de|Ortschrein|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/town shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) in the 17th year (1884). In the 11th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1936), it was raised to the status of {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Festivals == * Main Festival : Held on April 25. * Koshiki Ageya Festival (Yōya Matsuri) == Subsidiary Shrine == * Tenman-gū == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)|version=81271364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:川田神社]] [[de:Kawata Jinja]] [[ja:川田神社]] [[ja:川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)]] {{wikidata link|Q11479034}} [[Category:Buildings in Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:pages]]

Reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} |MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ikkensha-nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |OtherName = |Pilgrimage = |MainFestival = {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25|1=April 25}} |Ritual = Koshiki Ageya Festival (February 20) }} '''Kawata Jinja''' (川田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka, Shiga}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Yakawa|de|Ame-no-Yakawa|ja|天湯河板挙|lt=Ame-no-Yakawa|lt_ja=天湯川桁命|lt_de=Ame-no-Yakawa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Yakawa|1=Ame-no-Yukawata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q52319907}} – Ancestor deity of the Tottori-no-Muraji * Amekawatana no Mikoto (Enshrined together) * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|de|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Onamuchi no Mikoto|de|Onamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} == Shrine Crest == * Trailing wisteria (下り藤) == History == According to tradition, it was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. Listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|de|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|de|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is one of the ''ronsha'' (theories) regarding that identification. In the 11th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} (1726), the shrine was elevated to Shōichii (正一位) in {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shinkai|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. It served as the ''sōja'' of {{ill|Kurata-shō|de|Kurata-shō|ja|蔵田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurata-shō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1876), it was designated a ''sonsha'' ({{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}), then became a ''gōsha'' ({{ill|town shrine|de|Ortschrein|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/town shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) in the 17th year (1884). In the 11th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1936), it was raised to the status of {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Festivals == * Main Festival : Held on April 25. * Koshiki Ageya Festival (Yōya Matsuri) == Subsidiary Shrine == * Tenman-gū == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)|version=81271364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:川田神社]] [[de:Kawata Jinja]] [[ja:川田神社]] [[ja:川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)]] {{wikidata link|Q11479034}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

It served as the ''sōja'' of {{ill|Kurata-shō|de|Kurata-shō|ja|蔵田荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurata-shō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1876), it was designated a ''sonsha'' ({{ill|village shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}), then became a ''gōsha'' ({{ill|town shrine|de|Ortschrein|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/town shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}) in the 17th year (1884). In the 11th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} (1936), it was raised to the status of {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Festivals == * Main Festival : Held on April 25. * Koshiki Ageya Festival (Yōya Matsuri) == Subsidiary Shrine == * Tenman-gū == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)|version=81271364|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11479034|ja|川田神社|zh|川田神社|de|Kawata Jinja|ja|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
